subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning of law and social justice
Learn why laws are needed to protect workers
Understand the concept of minimum wages
Know about child labour and laws against it
Recognize the importance of safety laws in workplaces
Understand the role of government in enforcing laws

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Law and Social Justice
topics_covered: Why laws are important in society
student_activities: Students discuss why rules are necessary in society
Give examples of laws that protect people

lesson_plan.title: Workers and Their Rights
student_activities: List two rights that workers should have

lesson_plan.title: Minimum Wages
student_activities: Write the meaning of minimum wage
Discuss why workers should receive fair pay

lesson_plan.title: Child Labour Laws
student_activities: Identify jobs that children should not do
Discuss why education is important for children

lesson_plan.title: Workplace Safety
student_activities: List two safety measures in workplaces
Discuss how safety laws protect workers

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Chapter Summary
topics_covered: Importance of laws in protecting workers
student_activities: Write two ways laws help workers

teacher_tip: Use simple real-life examples such as workers in factories or shops to help students understand how laws protect people's rights.
